description: Local approved catalog for reviewed MCP servers. MCPace may auto-add approved/trusted entries during a no-query discovery sweep, then probe and isolate them before use. Teams can downgrade entries to review or blocked.

filesystem.notes: Path-scoped filesystem servers are useful but can leak or mutate files; isolate by client/project/chat unless proven read-only.
filesystem.title: Filesystem
filesystem.description: Path-scoped local filesystem MCP server for reading and writing project files.
filesystem.approvalReason: Curated starter entry: safe to auto-add to MCPace config, still probed and isolated before use; not a blanket guarantee about upstream package code.

fetch.notes: Usually stateless, but still review network egress and credential handling.
fetch.title: Fetch
fetch.description: Network fetch MCP server for retrieving web/API resources.
fetch.approvalReason: Curated starter entry: safe to auto-add to MCPace config, still probed and isolated before use; not a blanket guarantee about upstream package code.

time.title: Time
time.description: Small stateless time/date utility MCP server.
time.notes: Usually stateless; review package/source before enabling automatic install.
time.approvalReason: Curated starter entry: safe to auto-add to MCPace config, still probed and isolated before use; not a blanket guarantee about upstream package code.

sequential-thinking.title: Sequential Thinking
sequential-thinking.description: Stateful reasoning scratchpad-style MCP server; isolate per chat/session.
sequential-thinking.notes: Treat as stateful until live tools/list and usage evidence prove otherwise.
sequential-thinking.approvalReason: Curated starter entry: safe to auto-add to MCPace config, still probed and isolated before use; not a blanket guarantee about upstream package code.
